lessons.title: The 8-Dimension Business Diagnostic
lessons.description: Run the complete Growth Analyzer framework to map a business's full digital health in one session.
lessons.description_es: Ejecuta el marco completo del Growth Analyzer para mapear la salud digital completa de un negocio en una sesión.
lessons.content:
The 8-Dimension Diagnostic Framework

The Growth Analyzer is NetWebMedia's core diagnostic tool — a structured assessment of all eight dimensions that determine a business's digital growth capacity. The goal is not a comprehensive audit; it's rapid signal identification. Where are the highest-leverage opportunities? Where are the active revenue leaks? You should be able to complete this diagnostic in 2 hours and produce a prioritized action list in the third.

Dimension 1 — Digital Presence: Does the business have a functional, indexed website? Is it mobile-responsive? Does it pass Core Web Vitals? Is it listed in Google Business Profile and accurate? Score 0-10.

Dimension 2 — SEO Health: Check: organic traffic trend (Google Search Console), keyword rankings for core commercial terms, domain authority (Ahrefs/Semrush), backlink profile quality, and indexing status. Score 0-10.

Dimension 3 — Paid Acquisition: Is the business running paid search or social ads? What is the current ROAS? Is conversion tracking set up correctly? Is there a remarketing campaign running? Score 0-10.

Dimension 4 — Content Marketing: Frequency of content publication. Blog health (indexed, pillar/cluster structure, internal linking). Social media content consistency and engagement. Score 0-10.

Dimension 5 — Email/CRM: Does the business have an email list? What is the list health (open rate, click rate)? Is there a lead nurture sequence? CRM adoption and data hygiene. Score 0-10.

Dimension 6 — Conversion Rate: Landing page conversion rates. Lead form completion rates. Checkout abandonment rate (e-commerce). Average session duration and bounce rate. Score 0-10.

Dimension 7 — Reputation/Social Proof: Google review rating and volume. Response rate to reviews. Social proof on website. NPS or customer satisfaction data. Score 0-10.

Dimension 8 — Analytics/Measurement: Is GA4 installed and configured? Are conversion goals set up? Is there a regular reporting cadence? Can the business attribute revenue to marketing channels? Score 0-10.

  • Score each dimension independently before drawing conclusions — biases toward/against certain channels distort the diagnosis
  • A score below 4 on any dimension signals an active revenue leak — address these before optimizing higher-scoring dimensions
  • Use the scores to build a radar chart — visual gaps are easier for clients to process than lists of numbers
  • Rerun the diagnostic every 90 days to measure progress and identify new gaps created by growth

lessons.title: Reading the Signal: Prioritizing What Actually Moves Revenue
lessons.title_es: Leer la señal: priorizar lo que realmente mueve los ingresos
lessons.description: Interpret diagnostic data to identify the two or three changes that produce 80% of available revenue gains.
lessons.description_es: Interpreta los datos del diagnóstico para identificar los dos o tres cambios que producen el 80% de las ganancias de ingresos disponibles.
lessons.content:
Signal vs. Noise in Diagnostic Data

The 8-dimension diagnostic generates 80 data points. Not all of them matter equally. Signal reading is the skill that separates analysts who produce comprehensive reports from operators who drive revenue. Here's how to extract the signal.

The Two-Axis Prioritization Matrix: For each identified problem, assess it on two axes: Revenue Impact (how much revenue is being lost or left on the table by this problem?) and Time-to-Fix (how quickly can this be resolved with available resources?). The quadrant you want is high impact + fast fix — these are your sprint priorities.

Common High-Signal Patterns:

Pattern 1 — The Traffic-to-Lead Gap: Site getting reasonable organic traffic (2,000+/month) but fewer than 2% conversion to leads. Signal: CRO problem. The business has audience — it's losing them at the conversion point. Priority: landing page redesign, lead magnet introduction, form optimization.

Pattern 2 — The Lead-to-SQL Gap: Generating leads but close rate is under 10%. Signal: lead quality or nurture problem. Either the wrong people are entering the funnel, or there's no system to warm them before the sales conversation. Priority: lead scoring, email sequence build-out, ICP (ideal customer profile) refinement.

Pattern 3 — The Dark Funnel: Revenue is happening but can't be attributed to any channel. Signal: measurement problem. Marketing decisions are being made blind. Priority: GA4 configuration, UTM parameter audit, CRM-to-analytics integration.

Pattern 4 — The Single Channel Dependency: 80%+ of leads come from one channel. Signal: concentration risk. Any algorithm change, policy update, or competitive pressure on that channel could collapse revenue. Priority: diversification sprint — build the next highest-ROI channel to 20% of lead volume within 90 days.

The Signal Brief: After diagnosis, write a one-page brief: three findings, three recommendations, and one priority. The one priority is the single most impactful change available in the next 30 days. Most businesses can only execute one strategic change at a time — give them the right one.

  • Separate structural problems (platform, architecture) from execution problems (copy, creative, targeting) — they require different solutions and timelines
  • Always include a quantified opportunity estimate: "Fixing the cart abandonment rate from 75% to 60% at current traffic would generate approximately $12,400/month in recovered revenue"
  • Diagnose before prescribing — clients who receive recommendations without diagnostic context are skeptical; those who see the data first are committed
  • Review competitor diagnostics in the same industry — gaps become opportunities when you see what competitors are missing

lessons.title: Building the 90-Day Growth Plan
lessons.title_es: Construir el plan de crecimiento de 90 días
lessons.description: Translate diagnostic findings into a concrete, sequenced action plan with owners, deadlines, and success metrics.
lessons.description_es: Transforma los hallazgos del diagnóstico en un plan de acción concreto, secuenciado, con responsables, plazos y métricas de éxito.
lessons.content:
The 90-Day Growth Plan Template

A diagnostic without a plan is a report. A plan without execution is a proposal. The 90-Day Growth Plan bridges diagnosis and execution — it's the document that turns insights into accountable action.

Plan Structure:

Section 1 — Current State Summary (1 page): The 8-dimension scores, the top three gaps, and the revenue opportunity estimate. This section proves you've done the work and justifies the priorities in the plan.

Section 2 — The 90-Day Objectives: Three to five specific, measurable outcomes. Format: [Metric] from [Current] to [Target] by [Date]. Example: "Organic leads from 45/month to 90/month by August 1." No objectives without baselines and targets — vague goals produce vague results.

Section 3 — The Action Roadmap: Organized by 30-day sprint. Each sprint has: the primary initiative, 3-5 specific tasks under that initiative, the owner, and the success metric. Do not include more than one major initiative per sprint — focus compounds execution quality.

Sprint 1 (Days 1-30): Foundation fixes. Measurement setup, CRO quick wins, technical SEO issues. These changes have zero latency — results appear immediately.

Sprint 2 (Days 31-60): Demand generation activation. Campaign launches, content publishing, email sequence deployment. These changes take 2-4 weeks to produce measurable data.

Sprint 3 (Days 61-90): Optimization and scaling. Double down on Sprint 2 winners, kill underperformers, build scalable systems around what's working.

Section 4 — Budget and Resource Requirements: What this plan costs, broken down by channel investment, tool costs, and agency/contractor fees. Include the projected ROI at the revenue targets in Section 2. Budget transparency builds trust and prevents mid-plan conflicts over resource allocation.

  • Share the plan in a collaborative format (Google Docs or Notion) so clients can add comments and context — it increases their ownership of the plan
  • Build a KPI dashboard on day 1 of execution so all stakeholders can see progress in real time
  • Schedule a plan review at day 45 — early enough to course-correct, late enough to have meaningful data
  • Never build a plan that depends on decisions outside your control (e.g., "CEO must approve new brand positioning by day 15") — plan around controllable inputs

lessons.title: Using the NetWebMedia Growth Analyzer Tool
lessons.title_es: Usar la herramienta Growth Analyzer de NetWebMedia
lessons.description: Operate the Growth Analyzer audit tool end-to-end: input, scoring, output report, and client presentation.
lessons.description_es: Opera la herramienta de auditoría Growth Analyzer de principio a fin: entrada, puntuación, reporte de salida y presentación al cliente.
lessons.content:
Growth Analyzer Tool Walkthrough

The NetWebMedia Growth Analyzer is an AI-powered diagnostic tool that automates the 8-dimension analysis and produces a branded PDF report in under 5 minutes. This lesson covers how to operate the tool, interpret its output, and use the report in client conversations.

Input Requirements: The tool requires six inputs to generate a complete analysis: (1) business website URL, (2) primary industry/niche, (3) business location (city/state for local SEO analysis), (4) up to 3 core keywords the business wants to rank for, (5) social media profile URLs (optional but recommended), and (6) estimated monthly marketing budget. With these six inputs, the AI can audit technical SEO, content quality, page speed, local presence, social activity, and competitive positioning.

The Scoring Engine: Each of the 8 dimensions is scored by the AI based on real data pulled from public sources: Google PageSpeed for Core Web Vitals, Common Crawl data for SEO signals, social profile analysis for content metrics, and structured SERP analysis for competitive positioning. Scores are normalized to a 0-10 scale with detailed sub-scores visible in the full report.

Report Sections:

  • Executive Summary: Overall digital health score (0-100), top three opportunities, and estimated revenue impact of addressing the top opportunity.
  • Dimension Scorecard: Radar chart plus written commentary on each of the 8 dimensions.
  • Competitive Benchmark: How the business scores against the top 3 organic competitors for their primary keyword.
  • Recommendations Roadmap: 90-day action list, automatically prioritized by impact × feasibility.
  • NetWebMedia Services Match: Which NWM service tiers directly address the identified gaps.

Client Presentation Protocol: Lead with the overall score — businesses instinctively respond to a grade. Then walk through the lowest two dimension scores (not all eight — focus creates urgency). Present the top recommendation with its revenue impact estimate. Close with the 90-day roadmap and a services conversation.

  • Run the analyzer before every discovery call — it gives you specific, data-backed talking points that generic agency pitches lack
  • Use the competitive benchmark section as a mirror: clients respond to "your top competitor scores 7.2 in SEO; you score 3.1" more viscerally than abstract score discussions
  • Export the PDF and email it as a follow-up within 24 hours of every discovery call — it extends the conversation into the client's inbox
  • Re-run the analyzer 90 days after engagement start to show improvement — measurable progress justifies retainer renewal
